paper_title: Verified Detection and Prevention of Concurrency Anomalies in Multi-Agent Large Language Model Systems

problem_reconstruction.original_question: Multi-agent LLM systems share mutable state; what concurrency anomalies arise, how to detect/prevent them, and at what cost?
problem_reconstruction.restated_as: Given an operational regime where operation latency is dominated by neural inference rather than memory access, what is the formal landscape of consistency anomalies, and can mechanically verified runtime strategies prevent them with bounded cost?
problem_reconstruction.why_relevant: Existing consistency theory (database isolation levels, hardware memory models) assumes bounded operation latency. LLM inference creates a fundamentally different regime — 'read-generate-write' windows of seconds, not microseconds — that invalidates core assumptions of both lock-based and optimistic concurrency control.
key_assumptions_examined: All LLM invocations are deterministic with respect to their inputs (load-bearing assumption for formal verification)
Shared memory is a single logical store (no replication — A4 deferred)
Cross-record commit is atomic; within-record tool-effect commit order is not (A6 formalizes this gap)
No Byzantine or adversarial agents (trace-level writes are genuine commits)
Cost asymmetry is bounded, not 'cripplingly expensive order-of-magnitude'

methodology_reconstruction.approach: Three-layer formal-empirical architecture combining TLA+ specification, Verus mechanical verification, and controlled LLM-scale empirical measurement
methodology_reconstruction.layer_1_formal: TLA+ predicates for 4+1 anomalies (A1, A2, A3, A4 deferred, A6) verified by TLC model checking at small finite bounds (3 agents, 2 cells, 8 slots); TLAPS coherence check (11 theorems, 15 obligations); A1 structural lower bound (28 obligations)
methodology_reconstruction.layer_2_verification: Verus mechanical verification chain: (a) detector soundness+completeness (24 obligations), (b) three runtime safety theorems (40 obligations), (c) four spec-runtime refinements (84 obligations), (d) concurrent semantics lift (9 + 4 obligations), (e) exec-mode verified runtimes (49+7+9 obligations). Total: 274 curated, 295 full obligations, 0 errors
methodology_reconstruction.layer_3_empirical: Controlled LLM-scale pilot: 900 sessions (3 workloads × 3 runtimes × 100 sessions) on gpt-4o and Claude Sonnet 4.5, plus synthetic baselines (700 traces), cookbook-derived scenarios (600 sessions), wall-clock within-model study (270+270 sessions), live agent loop (cascade evaluation)
key_design_choices: Boolean lattice (Adya-style) rather than single hierarchy — captures non-totally-ordered relationships among anomalies
Verus for Rust verification rather than Coq/Isabelle — targets the executable runtime directly
Empirical cost measurement at LLM scale — tests the qualitative 'order-of-magnitude' intuition against real provider pricing
Dual-evidence strategy for each anomaly: formal (TLA+ predicate + TLC witness) AND empirical (pilot firing rate + cost)
Cross-model replication (gpt-4o + Claude Sonnet 4.5 + Llama-3.2 open-weights) — tests whether findings are provider-specific

analytical_framework.core_construct: Consistency Lattice L = (2^{A}, ⊆) where A = {A1, A2, A3, A6}; each model = subset of anomalies excluded; lattice is Boolean on 4 generators = 16 points
analytical_framework.chosen_mapping: L0(⊤) → L1(-A1) → L2(-A1-A3) → L3(-A1-A3-A6) → L4(⊥, excludes all four). This is one of 24 maximal chains.
analytical_framework.verification_chain: TLA+ predicates → Verus detector equivalence (sound+complete) → Verus runtime safety theorems → Verus refinement to deployed Rust → executable exec-mode verified runtimes → empirical cost/liveness measurement

threat_to_validity_mapping.construct_validity: Are the 5 anomalies exhaustive? Author acknowledges A4 is deferred to replication-aware model; A5 (LongGeneration) subsumed. Coverage is justified by the lattice structure (any new anomaly is a new generator dimension) rather than claimed completeness
threat_to_validity_mapping.internal_validity: TLC verification at small bounds (3 agents, 2 cells, 8 slots). Positive witnesses generalize upward; negative checks don't. The 274 Verus obligations use 2 axioms + 3 RustBelt stubs — not 'zero assume' in the strongest sense
threat_to_validity_mapping.external_validity: Two LLM providers (gpt-4o, Claude Sonnet 4.5), three workloads. Gemini, Mistral, open-weights cost not measured. Production prevalence not established — the 1-35-100% rates are workload-engineered
threat_to_validity_mapping.conclusion_validity: SSI cost-parity claim carefully bounded ('indistinguishable between sessions, not exact zero'). Cross-model cost comparison confounded by tokenization/pricing — only within-model claims made

key_findings.finding_1: A1 (Stale-Generation) exists in two forms — structural (guaranteed by workload design, 100% in edit-review) and empirical (workload-dependent, 1-35%)
key_findings.finding_2: Classical snapshot isolation is insufficient for A1 prevention — the write-skew pattern (Berenson et al., Cahill et al.) manifests in the agent setting as a 3% residual A1 rate from read-only operations that bypass SI validation
key_findings.finding_3: SSI prevents A1 at statistically zero token-cost overhead vs vanilla across both providers — the most cost-effective runtime strategy in low-to-moderate contention regimes
key_findings.finding_4: Pessimistic locking prevents A1 at 100% with bounded overhead (1.6×-2.3×) — challenging the 'orders-of-magnitude' intuition pervasive in the multi-agent LLM literature
key_findings.finding_5: A6 (Tool-Effect Reordering) exists in deployed code (LangGraph's ToolNode asyncio.gather), is invisible in chat transcripts, and is eliminated by a commit-order sequencer
key_findings.finding_6: Causal-cascade (A3) prevention is structural: the verified L2 runtime's cascade-abort discipline guarantees A3-freedom by construction, not by measured reliability
key_findings.finding_7: Operational materiality of A1 is sharply role-dependent: assessor roles diverge 100%, producer roles 0% — suggesting a role-aware advisory layer over verified detectors

formal_limitations: Single-store model excludes A4 (Split-View); replication-aware extension is deferred
Deterministic LLM assumption — probabilistic anomaly rate (P[stale read > threshold]) not established
TLC verification at small finite bounds — no inductive argument generalizing negative results to larger configurations
Sequential refinement semantics — multi-threaded mutual exclusion discharged as trust base assumption, not proof
empirical_limitations: Production anomaly prevalence not established — pilot rates are workload-engineered demonstration of capability, not epidemiological measurement
Two LLM providers only — Gemini, open-weights families not cost-measured
Cost measurement uses billed tokens, not wall-clock compute — SSI cost-parity finding reads best-case for SSI's commercial viability
Wall-clock study uses mock deterministic tools — real-tool latency and cross-call interference not included
scope_limitations: No Byzantine/adversarial agent model — hallucinated writes indistinguishable from stale reads at trace level
Single logical key-value store — message-passing architectures (AutoGen actor model) not directly modeled
Abort-and-retry cost asymmetry acknowledged but not formalized in the lattice itself
Chosen-chain ordering by operational reasoning, not empirical validation against deployed runtimes
